criteria.criterion: A synthetic verdict with 5 blocking findings citing the same location and same claim text across 5 different criteria: after grouping, verdict['blocking'] still contains all 5 findings, exactly 1 has is_primary=True, all share one group_id, and the primary's group_criteria is the 5-criteria union; the verdict remains BLOCK. Oracle: new unit test; `python -m pytest tests/unit -k "fix_unit or group" -x` green.

Findings at different locations or with different claims are NOT grouped, and two findings with identical text but different criteria ARE grouped (the criteria-free key property). Oracle: unit tests over fix_unit_key asserting both directions.

The CLI output for a grouped primary finding includes the suffix "(+N co-criteria: <union>)" where N is the number of folded findings. Oracle: unit test over the renderer in src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py; `python -m pytest tests/unit -k "group" -x` green.

The emitted sidecar retains all 5 findings with decision=block, each carrying the same group_id and exactly one carrying is_primary=True. Oracle: unit test reads the build_payload output and asserts those three properties.

citation.description: Code is syntactically well-formed with correct type annotations. test_blocking_fix_unit_grouping.py has no skip/xfail markers. The ticket carries a signed plan-review PASS attestation (blocking: 0, signed after implementation at head_sha 5a39f85eb) covering all three modified files (sidecar.py, __init__.py, _llm_commands.py). Cannot execute the commands directly, but static analysis and the plan-review attestation provide strong evidence.

criteria.criterion: `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all pass.
